text.title: ADA Title II Compliance 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the ada-title-ii-compliance-2026 methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Conformance target MUST be WCAG 2.1 AA, NOT 2.2; the DOJ April 2024 final rule explicitly references 2.1 AA.

rationale.source: 28 CFR Part 35 — DOJ Title II rule April 2024

rule.rationale: Legal text controls; aiming higher (2.2 AA) is acceptable practice but the audit MUST score against 2.1 AA to evidence Title II conformance.

rule.statement: Deadline MUST be set by jurisdiction population: April 24, 2026 (>=50,000) OR April 26, 2027 (<50,000). The report MUST state the bucket.
rule.rationale: Population-based deadlines are codified; missing the bucket invalidates the report.

rule.statement: Conformance scope MUST cover all public-facing digital services (web, mobile apps, kiosks); internal-only employee systems MUST be excluded from the Title II scope.
rule.rationale: Title II scope is public-facing; including internal systems inflates the report unnecessarily, excluding them is a finding gap.

rule.statement: Conformance MUST be evidenced by a Stage 1-5 accessibility-evaluation audit; a written self-attestation without audit evidence MUST NOT be accepted.
rule.rationale: Self-attestation has no defensible basis in litigation; audit evidence does.

rule.statement: Third-party widgets, fonts, embedded videos, and SaaS components hosted on public-facing surfaces MUST be in scope; vendor agreements MUST require WCAG 2.1 AA conformance.

rationale.source: DOJ Title II rule + 2025 enforcement letters

rule.rationale: DOJ rule does not exempt third-party content; excluding it is the most common compliance gap.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
